compiled_state.title: Debug-Everything Restructure and Fix-Bug Orchestrator Promotion
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context
Bugs accumulate across sprint sessions when sub-agents defer or misclassify issues. The current /dso:debug-everything skill wastes time on diagnostic and triage sub-agents when bugs already exist as tickets, and its sub-agent dispatch of /dso:fix-bug prevents fix-bug from using its own investigation sub-agents due to the nesting prohibition. Practitioners currently work around this by manually curating bug lists and running fix-bug sequentially.

## Success Criteria
1. When debug-everything is invoked with open bug tickets, it skips diagnostic/triage phases — no diagnostic or triage sub-agents dispatched when open bug tickets exist
2. fix-bug runs at the orchestrator level (not as a sub-agent) with full Agent tool access — investigation sub-agents complete without nesting errors
3. The validation→bug-fix loop has a configurable max iteration count (default 3, via debug.max_fix_validate_cycles in dso-config.conf) — after which remaining issues are reported as open tickets and orchestrator stops
4. Debug-everything asks at session start whether the session is interactive; when non-interactive, items requiring user input are deferred (bug left open with classification comment noting which gate flagged it) rather than blocking
5. In a dogfooding session with 3+ open bug tickets, the restructured pipeline completes the fix loop and the practitioner confirms it required fewer manual interventions than the prior workflow

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Two modes: bug-fix mode (skip diagnostic/triage, invoke fix-bug sequentially at orchestrator level) and validation mode (run diagnostic scan after known bugs are addressed, create tickets for new failures, loop back to bug-fix mode up to configured max). fix-bug's sub-agent guard is updated first as a story sequencing constraint before dispatch logic changes ship. Stories sequenced: guard update → dispatch restructure → validation loop → interactivity question.

## Scenario Analysis
- Unbounded loop (high): addressed by SC3 — configurable max iterations with default 3
- Non-interactive blocking (high): addressed by SC4 — interactivity question at session start
- Partial rollout guard mismatch (critical): guard update sequenced first in story ordering
- Sequential blocking (medium): accepted tradeoff for MVP — typical batch is 1-3 bugs

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 10
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none

comments.body: Adversarial review: 7 findings, 6 accepted. Full exchange: /tmp/workflow-plugin-886c4e92a94d7e0e/adversarial-review-2df5-72cb.json
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
Epic implementation complete: SC1-SC4 verified PASS by completion verifier. SC5 (dogfooding session with 3+ bugs) requires practitioner validation in a separate session — all code and tests are in place.

Summary of changes (11 files, 1129 insertions):
- fix-bug: Agent tool availability as primary context detection method
- debug-everything: Bug-Fix Mode (skip diagnostic/triage for open bugs)
- debug-everything: Validation Mode (fix→validate loop, max iterations configurable)
- debug-everything: Interactivity question + non-interactive deferral for 5 gates
- Config: debug.max_fix_validate_cycles=3
- Docs: CLAUDE.md + CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md updated
- Tests: 6 new test files (34 test functions total)
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
